title: Case when statement dealing with null logic return a wrong result.
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->

1. schema.

```sql
create table `t2`
(
    `vkey` integer,
    `c16`  double,
    primary key (`vkey`)
);
insert into `t2` (`vkey`, `c16`)
values (4, 1.73);
```



2. sql statement.

```sql
select case when
(
    select (
               ((null) in
                (select ((case when ((`t2`.`c16`) <> (-3.12)) then (`t2`.`vkey`) else (`t2`.`vkey`) end
                            ) is null))
                   ) is not null)
    from `t2`
)
then 11
else 22
end as `result`;
```





### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

MySQL and TIDB have different execution results in the above case.

The correct result in MySQL 8:

```SQL
mysql> select case when
    -> (
    ->     select (
    ->                ((null) in
    ->                 (select ((case when ((`t2`.`c16`) <> (-3.12)) then (`t2`.`vkey`) else (`t2`.`vkey`) end
    ->                             ) is null))
    ->                    ) is not null)
    ->     from `t2`
    -> )
    -> then 11
    -> else 22
    -> end as `result`;
+--------+
| result |
+--------+
|     22 |
+--------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```





### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

In TiDB v8.1.1:

```sql
MySQL [test]> select case when
    -> (
    ->     select (
    ->                ((null) in
    ->                 (select ((case when ((`t2`.`c16`) <> (-3.12)) then (`t2`.`vkey`) else (`t2`.`vkey`) end
    ->                             ) is null))
    ->                    ) is not null)
    ->     from `t2`
    -> )
    -> then 11
    -> else 22
    -> end as `result`;
+--------+
| result |
+--------+
|     11 |
+--------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```









### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->

tidb v8.1.1
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t3 (id INT, value FLOAT, status VARCHAR(10), PRIMARY KEY (id));
INSERT INTO t3 (id, value, status) VALUES (1, NULL, 'active');
INSERT INTO t3 (id, value, status) VALUES (2, 3.14, 'inactive');
SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM t3 WHERE value IS NULL) > 0 THEN 'Contains NULL' ELSE 'No NULLs' END AS null_check;
SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT (CASE WHEN value IS NOT NULL THEN id ELSE NULL END) FROM t3 WHERE status = 'active') IS NULL THEN 'No active IDs' ELSE 'Has active IDs' END AS active_id_check;
key_bug_features: Case when statement handling null logic incorrectly
